question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
note: He names a specific blocker to next-level scope — staying in execution details — which is a strong growth constraint with some self-awareness about the gap.

question.label: Execution details
question.name: Execution details — what keeps pulling you back into them?
question.description: Drills into the specific forces keeping Ahmed in tactical work instead of higher-level scope.

answer: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
note: Retreating into direct delivery when conversations turn political signals a strong growth blocker around influence at senior scope.

question.label: Political conversations
question.name: Political conversations — what makes them hard for you to stay in?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic behind his retreat from influence-heavy situations.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
note: Hesitation around shaping cross-org alignment signals a core next-level growth gap; [THREAD-DEFERRED] cross-org alignment hesitation not drilled because drill cap forced stage advance.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
note: He names confidence in senior forums as the stretch point and signals avoidance of visible senior-level exposure, indicating a strong growth gap and mild pullback in engagement.

question.label: Senior forums
question.name: Senior forums — what would help you show up there with more confidence?
question.description: Drills into the specific confidence gap he named in visible senior settings.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
note: He names a self-limiting absence of advocacy for opportunities, which signals strong growth stall and reduced proactive engagement.

question.label: Opportunities wanted
question.name: What opportunities do you actually want to be explicit about?
question.description: Turns his self-advocacy thread into a concrete picture of the scope and opportunities he wants next.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
note: He named a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is a strong positive growth signal.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
note: He names a concrete future-state move — delegating hands-on work to create room for bigger scope — which signals forward growth intent and active investment; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

question.label: Delegate more
question.name: What are you still holding that someone else could own?
question.description: Tests where he is still acting as an individual executor instead of leveraging the team.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
note: Names a missing growth structure — a clear contract with milestones — which signals absent development clarity and mild accountability strain.

question.label: Next experiment
question.name: What's one concrete shift you want to try before our next conversation?
question.description: Closes on a specific commitment that turns the growth plan into action.

note: [SKIP] no signal — planner bypassed, queue carried forward
